scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(String, context non-null, baseUri non-empty) returns node list via treeBuilder.parseFragment
scenarios.precondition: Use real HtmlTreeBuilder instance and Element context; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult:
List returned contains nodes corresponding to parsed fragment "

text

"; context.parent() unchanged
pseudocode: TEST TC01_O1 parseFragmentInput String overload with non-null context

String fragment = "

text

"; Element context = new Element("div"); String baseUri = "http://example.com";
assert result.size() == 1; assert result.get(0) instanceof Element; assert ((Element)result.get(0)).tagName().equals("p");

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(String, context null, baseUri empty) returns node list via treeBuilder.parseFragment
scenarios.precondition: Use real HtmlTreeBuilder; context null allowed
scenarios.expectedResult: List<Node> returned contains nodes for fragment "Hello"; context is null so global context used
pseudocode: TEST TC02_O1 parseFragmentInput String overload with null context
String fragment = "Hello"; Element context = null; String baseUri = "";

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(String, context non-null, baseUri null) returns node list and preserves fragment content
scenarios.precondition: Use real HtmlTreeBuilder; baseUri null tolerated
scenarios.expectedResult: List<Node> returned contains element for "<span/>"; calling node.baseUri() yields null or ""
pseudocode: TEST TC03_O1 parseFragmentInput String overload with null baseUri
String fragment = "<span/>"; Element context = new Element("div"); String baseUri = null;

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(Reader, context non-null, baseUri non-empty) returns node list
scenarios.precondition: Use StringReader as Reader; real HtmlTreeBuilder; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: List<Node> returned contains one TextNode with data "data"
pseudocode: TEST TC04_O2 parseFragmentInput Reader overload with non-null context
Reader fragment = new StringReader("data"); Element context = new Element("p"); String baseUri = "uri";

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(Reader, context null, baseUri non-empty) returns node list
scenarios.expectedResult: List<Node> returned contains element "a" for fragment "<a/>"
pseudocode: TEST TC05_O2 parseFragmentInput Reader overload with null context
Reader fragment = new StringReader("<a/>"); Element context = null; String baseUri = "http";

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(Reader, context any, baseUri any) throws UncheckedIOException when Reader IO fails
scenarios.precondition: Stub Reader to throw IOException on read; mocking Reader minimal
pseudocode: TEST TC06_IOE_O2 parseFragmentInput Reader overload IO exception
Reader fragment = new Reader(){ public int read(char[] cbuf,int off,int len)throws IOException{throw new IOException("fail");} public void close(){} }; Element context = null; String baseUri = "uri";

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(Reader) acquires and releases lock even on successful parse
scenarios.precondition: Use a custom TreeBuilder that records lock-held state; stubbed TreeBuilder for behavior
scenarios.expectedResult: lock was held during parse and released after; returned nodes list non-null
pseudocode: TEST TC07_lock_reentrancy_O2 verify lock usage in parseFragmentInput

TreeBuilder stub = new TreeBuilder(){override parseFragment(...){assert lock.isHeldByCurrentThread(); return Collections.emptyList();}}; Parser p = new Parser(stub); Reader fragment=new StringReader("<b/>");

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(Reader) releases lock when treeBuilder.parseFragment throws runtime exception
scenarios.precondition: Stub TreeBuilder.parseFragment to throw RuntimeException; stub described
scenarios.expectedResult: RuntimeException thrown; lock not held afterwards
pseudocode: TEST TC08_lock_release_on_exception_O2 parseFragmentInput releases lock on runtime exception
TreeBuilder stub = new TreeBuilder(){override parseFragment(...){throw new RuntimeException("err");}}; Parser p = new Parser(stub); Reader fragment=new StringReader("x");

scenarios.description: parseFragmentInput(String) with trackPosition enabled retains trackPosition flag after parse
scenarios.expectedResult: p.isTrackPosition() == true; returned nodes list non-null
pseudocode: TEST TC09_O1 parseFragmentInput preserves trackPosition flag
Parser p = Parser.htmlParser().setTrackPosition(true); String frag="<i/>"; Element ctx=new Element("div");
assert p.isTrackPosition(); assert result.get(0) instanceof Element;
